# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 146
- **Lines of code**: 44,532
- **Languages**: TypeScript (134), JSON (6), Markdown (3), Other (3)
- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-09T03:39:12.396Z

## Architecture Zones

### Analysis Engine (`analysis-engine`)

The core static-analysis pipeline: file inventory, import graph traversal, zone enrichment, LLM-assisted classification, output generation, and MCP server entry.

- Files: 40
- Cohesion: 0.62, Coupling: 0.38
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph-findings.ts, src/analyzers/classify.ts, src/analyzers/claude-client.ts, src/analyzers/context.ts, src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts (+14 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/analysis-engine/context.md`

### Analyze Pipeline CLI (`analyze-pipeline-cli`)

Orchestrates the end-to-end analyze command: phase sequencing, manifest management, convergence detection, git credential handling, PDF export, and all CLI output/error formatting.

- Files: 30
- Cohesion: 0.65, Coupling: 0.35
- Entry points: src/analyzers/manifest.ts, src/cli/commands/analyze-phases.ts, src/cli/commands/analyze.ts, src/cli/errors.ts, src/cli/output.ts (+1 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/analyze-pipeline-cli/context.md`

### Branch Workspace Reporting (`branch-workspace-reporting`)

Groups branch-work tracking utilities, risk scoring, workspace aggregation, PR report generation, schema definitions, and the package public API alongside co-located unit tests.

- Files: 29
- Cohesion: 0.40, Coupling: 0.60
- Entry points: src/analyzers/branch-work-classifier.ts, src/analyzers/risk-scoring.ts, src/cli/commands/workspace.ts, src/generators/pr-markdown-template.ts, src/schema/data-files.ts (+2 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/branch-workspace-reporting/context.md`

### Claude Tool Config (`claude-tool-config`)

Stores Claude Code permission settings and local overrides for the n-dx development environment.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/claude-tool-config/context.md`

### File Analyzer Implementations (`file-analyzer-implementations`)

Houses the concrete analyzer service implementations — callgraph, component, import, inventory, and route-detection — along with their unit tests and the shared path utilities they depend on.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.48, Coupling: 0.52
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph.ts, src/analyzers/components.ts, src/analyzers/imports.ts, src/analyzers/inventory.ts, src/util/paths.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/file-analyzer-implementations/context.md`

### Project Root (`project-root`)

Houses workspace-level documentation, build configuration, and project identity assets that define the monorepo structure.

- Files: 9
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/project-root/context.md`

### Schema Validation (`schema-validation`)

Contains the JSON schema validation module for sourcevision output files, alongside the end-to-end CLI tests and integration pipeline tests that exercise the full analysis workflow.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Entry points: src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/schema-validation/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`test-fixtures`)

Provides static sample projects (a Remix app and a small TypeScript project) used as realistic inputs for end-to-end and integration tests of the analysis pipeline.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/test-fixtures/context.md`

### Zone Detection Engine (`zone-detection-engine`)

Implements the Louvain community-detection algorithm, zone hashing for stability, and the zone assembly pipeline that transforms the import graph into named architectural zones.

- Files: 6
- Cohesion: 0.27, Coupling: 0.73
- Entry points: src/analyzers/zone-hash.ts, src/analyzers/zones.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/zone-detection-engine/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/cli/output.ts` (14 imports)
- `src/util/sort.ts` (12 imports)
- `src/analyzers/claude-client.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/cli/errors.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/schema/data-files.ts` (10 imports)
- `tests/unit/analyzers/zones-helpers.ts` (10 imports)
- `src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyzers/zones.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/util/paths.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyzers/inventory.ts` (7 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 57 files)
- `@n-dx/llm-client` (used by 23 files)
- `typescript` (used by 5 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `zod` (used by 2 files)
- `ora` (used by 1 files)
- `pdfkit` (used by 1 files)

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Zone exposes 19 concrete implementation files as its API surface with no facade or interface module — consumer zones are forced to import internal files directly, creating maximum blast radius for any internal restructuring. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- completion-reader.ts explicitly documents itself as a local copy of logic from branch-work-collector.ts — duplicated cross-zone logic with no enforcement mechanism to keep the two in sync; any fix to the original will silently miss the copy. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- branch-work-collector.ts and convergence.ts are domain services (not CLI commands) placed in the CLI zone — non-CLI consumers cannot reach these services without taking a transitive dependency on the CLI orchestration layer. [warning] (analyze-pipeline-cli)
- pr-markdown.ts (CLI command) directly imports branch-work-collector.ts from the analyzers path while branch-work-collector lives in the analyze-pipeline-cli zone — the import path and the zone boundary are misaligned, hiding that this is a CLI-to-CLI intra-zone call rather than a CLI-to-domain cross-zone call. [info] (analyze-pipeline-cli)
- branch-work-collector.ts is the canonical owner of parsing logic that has already been duplicated into analysis-engine/completion-reader.ts — hosting authoritative domain logic in a high-coupling catch-all zone creates pressure to fork rather than import, accelerating divergence. [warning] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- src/util/paths.ts is a utility misclassified into the analyzer-implementations zone by import affinity. As a cross-cutting utility imported by multiple zones, it belongs in a dedicated foundation or utility zone alongside sort.ts and schema/index.ts — the three hub utilities should share an owner zone. [warning] (file-analyzer-implementations)
- E2e and integration test files are co-located in the same Louvain community because both import validate.ts — CI parallelism and test reporting depend on these tiers being separately categorized. Mixing them in one zone obscures which test tier caught a regression. [info] (schema-validation)
- Zone contains 1 production file and 4 test files (80% test) — the zone boundary is a test-grouping artifact, not a domain boundary. The single production file (validate.ts) has no identity as a 'zone' and should be absorbed into a shared schema or foundation zone where it can be imported by the pipeline. [warning] (schema-validation)
- Fixture paths are consumed as string literals scattered across test files in multiple zones rather than through a centralized fixture registry or path-constants module. A single tests/fixtures/index.ts exporting named fixture paths would make relocation atomic and prevent path drift. [info] (test-fixtures)
- zones.ts is exercised by three separate unit test files each covering a distinct algorithmic concern (detection, size policy, subdivision) — this indicates zones.ts has at least three separate responsibilities. Splitting it into zones-detect.ts, zones-size-policy.ts, and zones-subdivide.ts would align source organization with the already-expressed test structure and improve zone cohesion from 0.27. [warning] (zone-detection-engine)
- ... and 1 more

### Patterns

- Production utilities (branch-work-*.ts, risk-scoring.ts) cohabiting with test helpers in the same zone causes the analyze-pipeline-cli zone to transitively depend on test infrastructure through its 13 inbound imports — separating production utilities from test helpers would break this unwanted coupling. [warning] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- The 15 outbound imports to analysis-engine and 2 inbound form a clean one-directional consumer relationship — no circular dependency exists between these two zones. [info] (file-analyzer-implementations)
- validate.ts has zero import edges to or from any production zone; it is only exercised through e2e and integration tests. If the analysis pipeline does not call validate() at the end of each run, malformed output files can reach consumers silently. [critical] (schema-validation)
- The two fixture sub-projects (remix-app, small-ts-project) are unrelated datasets with no shared imports; grouping them in one zone is a filesystem artifact. If a third fixture is added, consider a fixtures/index.ts manifest to keep fixture discovery centralized. [info] (test-fixtures)
- Three separate unit test files (zone-detection, zone-size-policy, zone-subdivision) map to distinct sub-domains within two production files — the test surface reveals a hidden split that should be made explicit in source organization before these concerns grow further. [warning] (zone-detection-engine)

### Relationships

- analysis-engine, analyze-pipeline-cli, and branch-workspace-reporting form a triangular circular dependency: analyzers→cli (7), cli→unit (13), unit→analyzers (29), analyzers→unit (5), unit→cli (3), cli→analyzers (20). All three zones are mutually reachable — this is a system-level cycle, not just three isolated pairwise inversions, and cannot be resolved by refactoring any single zone alone. [critical] (analysis-engine)
- High internal cohesion (0.65) coexists with active participation in circular dependencies — cohesion and external coupling are orthogonal properties; the CLI zone's boundary health requires coupling analysis, not cohesion metrics, to diagnose. [info] (analyze-pipeline-cli)
- route-detection.ts has no import edges to sibling analyzer files in this zone; it is grouped by filesystem proximity only and should be extracted to a dedicated route-analysis zone once zone boundaries are tightened. [warning] (file-analyzer-implementations)
- The schema-validation zone is correctly isolated from analyzers and cli zones for portability, but the absence of any inbound import from the pipeline entrypoint means validation is opt-in rather than enforced — a single import from the pipeline finalization step would close this gap. [warning] (schema-validation)
- Zero inbound edges from any production zone confirms correct isolation; verify that fixture paths are referenced via a shared constant in test helpers rather than duplicated path strings to reduce coupling to the filesystem layout. [info] (test-fixtures)
- zone-hash.ts has no outbound edges to zones outside zone-detection-engine; if it is only consumed within this zone it is a private module incorrectly treated as a zone boundary and should be marked internal. [info] (zone-detection-engine)

### Observations

- 19 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (analysis-engine)
- High cohesion (0.62) and low coupling (0.38) reflect a well-bounded core engine — the majority of cross-zone traffic flows in the correct direction (inward toward this zone). [info] (analysis-engine)
- Reverse imports from analyzers into the cli and unit zones (7 + 5 edges) suggest mild layering inversions; auditing these imports and pushing shared types down to a foundation module would improve the dependency graph. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- High coupling (0.6) — 29 imports target "analysis-engine" [warning] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- Branch-work utilities (classifier, filter, store) and risk-scoring form a coherent sub-cluster within this zone; they could be extracted into a dedicated 'branch-risk-analysis' zone if the codebase grows. [info] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- Coupling score of 0.6 meets the warning threshold; the zone imports from analyzers (29), cli (3), analyzers-2 (2), analyzers-3 (3), and e2e (2), indicating it acts as a cross-cutting hub rather than a focused module. [warning] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- Mixing src/public.ts (the package's public API entrypoint), schema type files, and unit test helpers in one community suggests the Louvain graph does not yet have enough edge weight to separate these concerns; manually anchoring schema and public API into a dedicated zone would help. [warning] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 2 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (claude-tool-config)
- Zero cohesion and coupling scores reflect that these JSON files have no import relationships; this is expected and correct for IDE config files. [info] (claude-tool-config)
- High coupling (0.52) — 15 imports target "analysis-engine" [warning] (file-analyzer-implementations)
- ... and 22 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "analysis-engine" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analysis-engine)
- Zone name 'analysis-engine' does not match its source directory 'src/analyzers/' — align them by either renaming the zone to 'analyzers-core' or restructuring source files under 'src/analysis-engine/' so IDE navigation, zone references in documentation, and Louvain clustering inputs are all consistent. [info] (analysis-engine)
- claude-client.ts provides direct LLM API access from within the core computation zone with no interface abstraction — extract an ILLMClient interface and inject it as a dependency so unit tests can substitute a stub and the analysis engine zone no longer carries an implicit external network dependency. [warning] (analysis-engine)
- Zone "analyze-pipeline-cli" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analyze-pipeline-cli)
- convergence.ts is a stateless pure-function algorithm placed inside the CLI orchestration zone — move it to the analysis-engine zone or a shared foundation module so its logic is reachable without a CLI layer dependency, and the CLI zone's semantic boundary remains limited to command orchestration. [warning] (analyze-pipeline-cli)
- export-pdf.ts and git-credential-helper.ts have no relationship to the analyze pipeline lifecycle — their presence violates the zone's stated semantic scope; move them to an 'ancillary-cli-commands' zone or equivalent to preserve the zone's architectural focus and prevent the 'analyze-pipeline' name from becoming misleading. [info] (analyze-pipeline-cli)
- Zone "branch-workspace-reporting" has files across 10 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- This zone has BOTH high coupling (0.6) AND low cohesion (0.4) — the only zone in this batch to exhibit both simultaneously. Prioritize splitting: move src/public.ts and schema types to a dedicated zone, and move branch-work utilities to a 'branch-risk-analysis' zone, to break the fragile catch-all structure before further features are added. [warning] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- src/public.ts co-located with branch-workspace internals forces every package consumer to transitively couple to this high-coupling zone — extract public.ts to its own dedicated zone (e.g. 'package-public-api') so the public API surface is independent of the branch reporting internals and can be changed without affecting branch-workspace coupling metrics. [warning] (branch-workspace-reporting)
- src/analyzers/ contains files from 5 zones (analysis-engine, analyze-pipeline-cli, branch-workspace-reporting, file-analyzer-implementations, zone-detection-engine) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- ... and 22 more

| `tsconfig.json` | config |  | project-root |
| `tsconfig.tsbuildinfo` | config |  | project-root |
| `vitest.config.ts` | config |  | project-root |

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
